zh.lifeSummary: 當Edward Hewitt於1446年出生在英國英格蘭可瓦爾聖 可利爾時，他的父親James Henry Hewitt26歲，母親Hannah Shingleston20歲。他於1460年之後與Rachell Pope在英國結婚。他們至少是6個兒子和5個女兒的父母。他去世於1525年4月9日在英國可瓦爾沃爾格根享年79歲。

nameMeanings.name: Hewitt
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: from the Middle English personal name Hewet, Huet, Hughet, Howet, pet forms of Hugh (Middle English Hewe, Hue; see Hugh and compare Hew ). The spelling Hughet stood for both Huet and the synonymous Huget; see Huggett . This surname has also been established in Ireland since the 14th century.

habitational name from Hewitts in Chelsfield or Hewitts in Willesborough (both Kent), both named from Old English hīewett ‘cutting’, denoting a place where trees had been cut down. Here there lived families called de la Hewatte (1270), de la hewett (1301), and atte Hewete (1338). The name may also be topographic for someone who lived in a newly made clearing in a wood.

variants.name: Hewitson
meanings: English (northern): patronymic from the personal name Hewet; see Hewitt .

variants.name: Huett
meanings: German (Hütt): status name for someone living in a hut or owning a small shop, Middle High German hütte, or a habitational name from any of several places called Hütt or Hütte. Compare Hutt .

variants.name: Hugh
meanings: English, Welsh, and Scottish (of Norman origin): from the Old French personal name Hue, from ancient Germanic Hugo (ultimately derived from hugi- ‘mind, spirit’). This was a very popular name in England after the Norman Conquest, partly due to the reputation of Saint Hugh of Lincoln (1140–1200), who was born in Burgundy and who established the first Carthusian monastery in England.
Scottish and Irish: adopted as an Anglicized form of the Gaelic personal name Aodh, a derivative of a Gaelic word meaning ‘fire’. See Hughes .
Chinese: variant Romanization of the surnames 邱 and 丘, based on their Hakka pronunciation; see   Qiu   1 and 2.

variants.name: Huet
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Marcel, Andre.
French: from a pet form of the personal name   Hue   ‘Hugh’.

nameMeanings.name: Edward
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + weard ‘guard’. This has been one of the most successful of all Old English names, in frequent use from before the Conquest to the present day, and even being exported into other European languages. It was the name of three Anglo-Saxon kings and has been borne by eight kings of England since the Norman Conquest. It is also the name of the youngest son of Queen Elizabeth II . The most influential early bearer was King Edward the Confessor ( ?1002–66 ; ruled 1042–66 ). In a troubled period of English history, he contrived to rule fairly and (for a time at any rate) firmly. But in the latter part of his reign he paid more attention to his religion than to his kingdom. He died childless, and his death sparked off conflicting claims to his throne, which were resolved by the victory of William the Conqueror at the Battle of Hastings. His memory was honoured by Normans and English alike, for his fairness and his piety. Edward's mother was Norman; he had spent part of his youth in Normandy; and William claimed to have been nominated by Edward as his successor. Edward was canonized in the 12th century, and came to be venerated throughout Europe as a model of a Christian king.

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Edward Hewitt was born in 1446, in St Cleer, Cornwall, England, United Kingdom, his father, James Henry Hewitt, was 26 and his mother, Hannah Shingleston, was 20. He married Rachell Pope after 1460, in England. They were the parents of at least 6 sons and 5 daughters. He died on 9 April 1525, in Warleggan, Cornwall, England, at the age of 79.

Il a épousé Rachell Pope après 1460, à Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 6 fils et 5 filles. Il est décédé le 9 avril 1525, à Warleggan, Cornouailles, Angleterre, à l’âge de 79 ans.

ko.lifeSummary: Edward Hewitt(이)가 1446년 영국 잉글랜드 코른월 스트 클리어에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 James Henry Hewitt(은)는 26세였고, 그의 어머니 Hannah Shingleston(은)는 20세였다. 그는 1460년 이후 영국에서 Rachell Pope(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1525년 4월 9일 영국 코른월 워레건에서 79세의 나이로 사망했다.
